betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Fractioning Tower§f

§aProcess refinery products into more refined fractions — no power required, just stack them up for speed!§f

The Fractioning Tower is a key machine used in all stages of oil processing. After the §bOil Refinery§f produces its four output fractions, the fractioning tower further breaks them down into more useful products like §bDiesel§f, §bKerosene§f, §bHeating Oil§f, and §bIndustrial Oil§f.

§6Operation§f:
§9Right-click§f the bottom-most block of the tower with the §bFluid Identifier§f of the fluid you wish to process. The tower will passively convert the input fluid into refined outputs without consuming power.

§6Recipes (per fluid type)§f:
§b• Heavy Oil§f (§e500mB§f): → §e150mB§f Bitumen + §e350mB§f Industrial Oil.
§b• Naphtha§f (§e250mB§f): → §e150mB§f Diesel + §e100mB§f Heating Oil + §e90mB§f Kerosene.
§b• Light Oil§f (§e150mB§f): → §e60mB§f Diesel.
§b• Industrial Oil§f (§e350mB§f): → §e210mB§f Heating Oil + §e140mB§f Engine Lubricant.

§6Stacking§f:
Several fractioning towers can be stacked atop one another to speed up processing. Vertically stacked towers push the input fluid up the stack while allowing output fractions to flow downwards. Connect pipelines to the bottom of the stack for maximum throughput.

§bFractioning Tower Separators§f are decorative blocks that allow you to visually separate stacked towers.

§eTip§f: The fractioning tower is entirely passive — no power, no heat, no moving parts. Build multiple parallel setups to process all of your refinery's output streams simultaneously. Focus on §bNaphtha§f processing first for §bDiesel§f and §bKerosene§f.
